title: strychnos unguacha
intro: Strychnos unguacha, also known as the Poison Nut, is a plant species found in tropical Africa that belongs to the Loganiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Strychnos unguacha include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and a treatment for intestinal parasites. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
